abstract: Pre-flight checklist that catches the dozen shapes of terminal freeze, approval-dialog miss, and cross-agent collision before they happen, framed as a surf check before paddling out. Twelve Tier-1 reject clauses enumerate the syntax-shape and shape-of-failure patterns that freeze IDE terminals when an LLM composes a run_command tool call; seven Tier-2 audits catch what survives a Tier-1 pass. Framing principle: permission prompts are not a durable security model. Anchors on Anthropic's Claude Code auto-mode disclosure (users approve 93% of permission prompts) and the Penligent Sandboxes for Coding Agents analysis. Env-agnostic fork of weijia-89/safe-terminal; ships the skill body and metadata only, with the runtime wrapper available in the sibling repo. Loaded always-on; multi-agent installable via skills.sh.
